Article.ArticleTitle: Effectiveness of resistance training and pain reprocessing therapy in female flight attendants with chronic low back pain: a study protocol for a randomized controlled trial.

Abstract.AbstractText: This study protocol presents the design of a randomized controlled trial to evaluate the effect of an 8-week multimodal intervention including resistance training alone versus combined resistance training with motor skills training and pain reprocessing therapy on lumbar range of motion (ROM), pain, mental well-being in female flight attendants aged between 20 and 40 years with chronic low back pain (LBP).

Based on findings from an a priori power analysis, a total of 60 flight attendants diagnosed with LBP will be enrolled and randomly assigned to one of the following experimental groups: (1) resistance training alone, (2) resistance training combined with motor skill exercises, (3) resistance training with pain reprocessing therapy, (4) resistance training with motor skill exercises and pain reprocessing therapy. Participants will be assessed at baseline, after 8 weeks (post-intervention), and 3 months post-intervention (follow-up, detraining).

This randomized controlled trial will provide valuable insights into the effectiveness of resistance training alone and in combination with motor skills training and pain reprocessing therapy for managing chronic low back pain in flight attendants.
The results of this study may help researchers and physiotherapy practitioners to improve clinical outcomes in female flight attendants with LBP.
The http://www.irct.ir/ identifier is IRCT20220804055617N1 (Registration date 2022-12-17).

OtherAbstract.AbstractText: PLAIN LANGUAGE SUMMARYIMPROVING BACK HEALTH IN FLIGHT ATTENDANTS: TESTING EXERCISE AND PAIN REPROCESSING APPROACHESLow back pain (LBP) is a common problem among flight attendants, caused by long periods of standing, heavy lifting, and repetitive movements during flights. LBP can also be worsened by stress, fear of movement, and unhelpful beliefs about pain. This pain can limit daily activities, reduce quality of life, and make work more difficult.This study aims to see how an 8-week exercise program can help reduce LBP in female flight attendants. Participants will be randomly placed into one of four groups: one doing resistance training alone, another doing resistance training with motor skills exercises, a third doing resistance training with pain management techniques, or a group that combines all three approaches. Resistance training helps strengthen the muscles that support the back, motor skills exercises improve movement control, and pain management techniques teach people how to change their thoughts and reactions to pain.Researchers will assess pain levels, back flexibility, balance, muscle endurance, and mental factors like fear of movement and confidence in handling pain. These assessments will take place before the program starts, right after the 8 weeks, and again three months later.We believe that combining resistance training with motor skills and pain management techniques will lead to better improvements in pain, flexibility, and mental health compared to resistance training alone. The findings from this study may help flight attendants manage their back pain more effectively and assist healthcare providers in recommending better treatment options for them.
